note: B25 keeps B2 boundary/rim auxiliary supervision and RBSG-lite unchanged. It inserts a lightweight large-kernel inverted bottleneck refiner on the final decoder feature before B2's pre-boundary/pre-rim heads and RBSG-lite. This provides local-to-long decoder context without adding another boundary/rim gate or output-logit rescue branch.

design_reflection.why_decoder: final decoder features are closest to pixel-level caries decisions and have only 16 channels, so large-kernel context is cheap
design_reflection.why_before_rbsg: B2's RBSG-lite can recalibrate the refined decoder feature using its established boundary/rim priors
design_reflection.why_not_dynamic_skip: B10 dynamic skip selection did not beat B2; B25 uses a fixed residual block instead of hard branch selection
design_reflection.why_not_channel_only: B21 channel-only IFE learned scale 0.0, so B25 uses spatial depthwise large kernels with near-identity residual initialization

design_references.title: CMUNeXt-style large-kernel CNN modules for medical image segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: large-kernel depthwise context with efficient inverted bottleneck

design_references.title: EMCAD: Efficient Multi-scale Convolutional Attention Decoding for Medical Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: decoder-side lightweight convolutional context can improve segmentation without replacing the encoder backbone

design_references.title: PRAD: A Benchmark Dataset for Periapical Radiograph Analysis and Diagnosis
design_references.adopted_idea: dental periapical radiographs favor lightweight CNN-compatible modules over full backbone replacement

lkdr.position: final SMP decoder feature before pre-boundary/pre-rim heads and RBSG-lite
